mappings.rationale: Directly covers general wave phenomena, wave classification, superposition, diffraction, interference, and all types of waves discussed in this section.
Directly covers sound waves, their properties, shock waves, sources and reception of sound, and acoustical applications discussed in this section.
Directly covers light waves, wave theory of light, diffraction, interference, polarization, dispersion, and quantum theory of light central to this section.
Covers wave motion, electromagnetic waves, sound, optics, and the fundamental properties of waves discussed in this section.
Relevant to electromagnetic wave generation, magnetic properties of materials interacting with electromagnetic radiation, and microwave sources.
Covers the quantum theory of light, wave-particle duality, the photon, and the uncertainty principle discussed in this section.
Relevant to gamma rays as part of the electromagnetic spectrum and the sources of gamma radiation from radioactive nuclei.
Covers the historical development of wave theory, optics, and the wave-particle debate that shaped modern understanding of light.
Relevant to geometrical optics, optical systems, lens design, diffraction, and the focusing and imaging of light waves in this section.
Covers the interaction of waves with matter, absorption, dispersion, and the propagation of electromagnetic waves through media.
Covers the wave equation, mathematical description of wave motion, and the space-time analysis of wave phenomena.
Relevant to nonlinear wave phenomena and complex wave interactions in turbulent and chaotic systems.
Relevant to X-ray diffraction and the interaction of waves with crystalline structures discussed in the section's treatment of electromagnetic waves.
Relevant to the velocity of light, its measurement, and the role of light speed in special relativity discussed in this section.
Covers the wave-particle duality, quantum theory of light, and foundational questions about the nature of waves and particles.
Relevant to gamma rays, their sources from radioactive nuclei, and their place in the electromagnetic spectrum.
Relevant to the symmetry properties of waves including polarization states and the mathematical structure of wave superposition.
Relevant to the measurement of wave properties including frequency, wavelength, amplitude, and the velocity of light.
Relevant to the interaction of light with solids, phonon waves in crystals, and the propagation of waves through condensed media.
Relevant to the development of electromagnetic wave theory and the historical understanding of light as electromagnetic radiation.
Relevant to seismic wave propagation and the behaviour of waves at boundaries, including reflection and refraction discussed in this section.

mappings.vsiAuthor: David George Bowers, Emyr Martyn Roberts
mappings.rationale: Relevant to water waves, wave phenomena, and the behaviour of waves in oceans and at boundaries.
Relevant to water waves, their properties and propagation, as a fundamental example of wave motion discussed in this section.
